Reason: This direction best combines digital detox, habit formation, emotional reward, and daily reflection without becoming a generic habit tracker. App store localization supports adding localized metadata and app languages without necessarily launching separate apps.
Context: After Batch 1 competitor research and top 10 deep dive, the user confirmed preference for the one sec + Forest + Daylio + Finch direction. The user also wants both Korean and English versions eventually, with global distribution potential.
Options Considered: 1. Generic habit tracker; 2. Strict digital blocker; 3. Planner/timeline app; 4. Meaningful-day recovery app combining impulse intervention, emotional reward, low-friction reflection, and warm self-care; 5. Separate Korean and English apps; 6. One app with localization and separate marketing assets.
Final Decision: Proceed with LifeOS MVP v0.1 as a meaningful-day recovery app based on one sec + Forest + Daylio + Finch. Plan bilingual Korean/English localization from the beginning, preferably as one app with localized in-app copy and localized store metadata, while allowing separate Korean/English marketing channels later.
Decision: LifeOS MVP v0.1 direction and bilingual localization principle
